behaviors.description: The system analyzes only the lines that changed in the PR, not entire files
behaviors.justification: The failure is in diff parsing and scoping logic. A unit-level mock of the diff would hide whether the system correctly filters changed vs. unchanged lines. You need the diff parsing code wired to the analysis code with realistic diff input. Real GitHub infrastructure is not needed—the failure boundary is in code logic.
behaviors.test_description: Create a test with a realistic multi-file diff fixture containing changed lines, unchanged content, additions, and deletions. Wire the diff parser to the review analyzer. Verify that the analysis only includes findings for changed lines, with no findings for unchanged sections. Use edge cases like changes at file start/end and complex hunks to ensure scoping is robust.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test runs in-process with fixture diffs and does not require external infrastructure. Verifies the boundary between diff parsing and analysis scoping.

behaviors.description: Inline review comments are posted at the correct line numbers in the pull request diff view
behaviors.justification: The failure is in line number mapping logic (translating code positions to diff positions, old vs. new file lines). This can be caught by wiring diff parsing, finding generation, and API-calling code together with a mocked GitHub API. You verify the API calls contain correct line numbers without needing real GitHub. Unit tests would not catch position mapping errors; System testing is not strictly required because the failure is in mapping logic, not API behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Create a test with a diff fixture containing known line number changes in multiple files. Generate findings at those positions. Mock the GitHub API comment-posting method and capture requests. Verify each request contains correct line numbers, commit references, and file paths. Test edge cases: changes at line 1, file end, multiple edits in the same hunk, and across different files.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test wires together diff parsing, finding generation, and API code with mocked GitHub API. Verifies line mapping without real GitHub.

behaviors.description: The code review agent detects a real defect that was intentionally introduced in a test pull request
behaviors.justification: The failure depends on LLM non-determinism—whether the model understands the code pattern and correctly identifies the bug. Mocking the LLM to return 'bug found' would always pass, hiding real failures where the LLM misses the bug. The model may produce different results on different runs. This requires a real LLM call against a known buggy code snippet to catch whether the agent actually performs code understanding.
behaviors.test_description: Create a test PR diff containing a known introduced bug (e.g., missing null check, incorrect loop condition, resource leak, unhandled exception). Call the agent with the real LLM backend to analyze the diff. Verify that the returned findings include at least one correct identification of the known bug. Run multiple times if necessary to ensure the bug is identified reliably (target: 8+ out of 10 runs). Document the bug pattern for reproducibility.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test directly depends on real LLM behavior and captures whether the agent performs actual code analysis, not mocked analysis.

behaviors.description: The top-level summary comment lists only findings that have a corresponding inline comment posted
behaviors.justification: The failure is a consistency bug between two code paths: finding generation and inline comment posting. This requires wiring together finding generation, inline comment creation, and summary generation code. Mocking the GitHub API allows you to capture all comment-posting calls and verify they match summary findings. The test can use fixtures for findings; the failure boundary is in consistency logic, not external infrastructure or LLM behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Wire together finding generation, inline comment posting, and summary generation logic. Mock the GitHub API to capture all comment-posting call requests. Create a scenario with multiple findings across different files, including duplicate findings and edge cases. Verify the summary comment lists exactly the findings posted as inline comments—no orphaned findings in the summary, no missing inline comments. Assert that the set of inline comment findings equals the summary finding set.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test runs in-process with mocked API, capturing consistency invariant between inline comments and summary without external infrastructure.

behaviors.description: The complete workflow—reading a diff, analyzing it, posting inline comments, and posting a summary—executes successfully on a real pull request
behaviors.justification: The behavior explicitly requires 'a real pull request' and 'without manual intervention'. Real GitHub infrastructure is necessary to verify end-to-end behavior. Mocks would miss real-world failures: network latency, API rate limiting, unexpected GitHub response formats, state changes during execution, and PR-specific edge cases. Testing against mocked GitHub would pass while real execution fails, making System-level testing (real GitHub API) the minimum.
behaviors.test_description: Create or use a test pull request in a test GitHub repository. Invoke the agent with the real GitHub API credentials to execute the full review workflow: fetch the PR diff, analyze it, post inline comments, post the summary comment. Verify: (1) diff fetches successfully, (2) inline comments post at valid positions, (3) summary posts without error, (4) no mid-workflow failures occur, (5) comments are visible and correct when fetched back from GitHub. Clean up test comments after execution. Document test PR setup, required permissions, and any rate-limit handling.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test requires real GitHub API and a controlled test PR to execute the full workflow and catch real-world failures that in-process mocks would miss.
